headline: PepsiCo Poppi Deal Tests Functional Beverage Push And Valuation Gap
summary: PepsiCo (NasdaqGS:PEP) has acquired prebiotic soda brand Poppi in a deal valued at $1.95 billion. Poppi is being launched in the UK, expanding PepsiCo's reach in functional, health focused beverages. The move adds a low sugar, high fiber beverage line aimed at younger, health conscious consumers. PepsiCo, trading at around $160.78, is adding Poppi to its portfolio at a time when investors are watching how large beverage companies respond to changing consumer tastes. Over the past year,...

headline: Has PepsiCo (PEP) Share Weakness Created A Fresh Opportunity For Patient Investors
summary: If you are wondering whether PepsiCo shares still justify their price tag, this article will walk through what the current numbers might be saying about value. PepsiCo last closed at US$160.78, after a 7 day return of a 2.5% decline, a 30 day return of a 5.7% decline, and gains of 13.0% year to date and 9.8% over the last year, with a 3 year return of 2.5% and a 5 year return of 40.1%. These moves come as PepsiCo continues to attract attention as a major global food and beverage brand, with...

headline: Why Utz (UTZ) Stock Is Falling Today
summary: Shares of snack food company Utz Brands (NYSE:UTZ) fell 4.1% in the afternoon session after sentiment weakened following negative results from peer Campbell Soup Company, which lowered its full-year sales and profit outlook due to underperformance in its snacks division.

headline: Poppi founder on TikTok, Super Bowl ads, and her return to Shark Tank
summary: For years, venture capitalists have been skeptical of beverage startups, citing thin margins and brutal distribution as reasons most brands never break out. But a new wave of “functional soda” companies has been challenging that assumption, including Poppi, the prebiotic soda brand that grew from a kitchen experiment into a $1.95 billion acquisition by PepsiCo. On this episode of […]

headline: 1 S&P 500 Stock for Long-Term Investors and 2 Facing Challenges
summary: The S&P 500 (^GSPC) is home to the biggest and most well-known companies in the market, making it a go-to index for investors seeking stability. But not all large-cap stocks are created equal - some are struggling with slowing growth, declining margins, or increased competition.

headline: Campbell’s Cuts Outlook as Snack Sales Fall
summary: Campbell’s cut its fiscal-year outlook as it reported a decline in second-quarter sales, dragged down by weak demand for its snacks.

headline: How Celsius is building brands — not just beverages — as growth surges
summary: Chief Brand Officer Kyle Watson explains how the company is working to individualize its portfolio, which now includes brands Alani Nu and Rockstar.

headline: Young Africans score higher than Americans on 'mind health,' new study finds: Wealth ‘not an indicator’ of well-being
summary: A new Sapien Labs study found that young people in Ghana and Nigeria are much more able to function than those in the U.S.
